chunks.en: When it comes to my to-do list, I jot everything down in Notion.
chunks.ja: やることリストに関しては、私はすべてをNotionに書き留めています。

chunks.en: The way I decide what comes first is by looking at deadlines and impact.
chunks.ja: 何を最初にやるか決める方法は、締め切りと影響度を見ることです。

chunks.en: The most satisfying part is ticking off a task once I've finished it.
chunks.ja: 一番満足できる瞬間は、終わったタスクにチェックを入れるときです。

chunks.en: As for unfinished tasks, I just carry them over to the next day.
chunks.ja: 終わらなかったタスクについては、翌日にそのまま繰り越します。

outline: Opening: where I keep my to-do list
Point 2: the joy of checking things off

modelTalk.text: Honestly, I'm a bit old-school when it comes to my to-do list. I jot everything down in a notebook, though I back it up in an app too. That way, I never lose track of anything. When I plan my day, I look at deadlines first and tackle whatever's most urgent. If two things feel equally important, I go with the one that's quicker. There's nothing quite like ticking off a finished task. That little checkmark honestly makes my day, and it keeps me motivated. Sometimes I even add a task I've already done, just to cross it out. As for the stuff I don't get through, I don't beat myself up about it. I simply roll it over to tomorrow's list and bump it up the priority. If I'd left it floating in my head, I'd probably forget it. Writing it down keeps my mind clear.

chunks.en: When it comes to managing my to-do list, I actually jot everything down in Notion.
chunks.ja: To-Doリストの管理について言うと、実は私はすべてをNotionに書き留めています。

chunks.en: The way I decide what comes first is by tackling the most urgent bug fixes early.
chunks.ja: 何を最初にやるか決めるやり方は、一番緊急なバグ修正を早めに片付けることです。

chunks.en: To be honest, the most satisfying part is ticking off a task once it's fully done.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、一番の快感はタスクが完全に終わったときにチェックを入れる瞬間です。

chunks.en: As for unfinished tasks, I usually carry them over to the next morning without stressing.
chunks.ja: 終わらなかったタスクについては、たいてい焦らず翌朝に持ち越すようにしています。

outline: Opening: where I write tasks down (Notion)
Point 1: how I prioritize urgent work first
Point 2: the satisfaction of checking things off
Wrap-up: rolling unfinished tasks to the next day

modelTalk.text: To be honest, keeping my to-do list under control is half the battle at work. I jot everything down in a plain notes app on my phone. Nothing fancy, actually — pen and paper would probably work too. Then I decide what comes first by asking myself what's urgent versus what just feels loud. Deadlines win, obviously, but I try not to let small stuff pile up. That said, the best bit is ticking things off. There's this weird little rush when I cross something out. It's silly, but it keeps me going, you know? As for whatever I don't finish, I don't beat myself up about it. I just roll it over to tomorrow's list, near the top. Sometimes I realise a task's been sitting there for days, and honestly, that's a hint it's not that important. So I either knuckle down and do it, or I let it go.
